check: SPF record covers every sending service
description: The sending domain's SPF record includes every provider that legitimately sends as it (transactional, marketing, support tools), stays under the 10-DNS-lookup limit, and ends ~all.
why_it_matters: SPF failures or lookup-limit overflows silently push mail to spam

check: DKIM signing enabled per provider with rotated keys
description: Every sending provider has its DKIM CNAME/TXT records installed and verified; keys are 2048-bit; old provider keys are removed when a provider is offboarded.
why_it_matters: Unsigned mail fails modern inbox providers' authentication requirements outright

check: DMARC published and progressed beyond p=none
description: A DMARC record exists with rua reporting; after monitoring alignment, policy moves p=none → p=quarantine → p=reject on a planned schedule.
why_it_matters: DMARC at reject is what actually stops others spoofing your domain — p=none forever is just logging

description: The provider's default bounce domain is replaced with a custom aligned subdomain (e.g. pm-bounces.product.com) so SPF/DMARC alignment passes.
why_it_matters: Misaligned return-path quietly fails DMARC alignment even with SPF/DKIM individually passing

description: If sending 5k+/day to Gmail: SPF+DKIM+DMARC all pass, one-click unsubscribe present (EM014), and spam-complaint rate held under 0.3% (ideally 0.1%).
why_it_matters: Since Feb 2024 these are enforced requirements — violators get rate-limited or rejected outright

check: Complaint and bounce rates monitored with alerts
description: Dashboards/alerts on complaint rate (>0.1% warning, >0.3% incident) and hard-bounce rate (>2% investigate); Google Postmaster Tools connected for domain reputation.
why_it_matters: These two numbers are your sender reputation — finding out late means weeks of recovery

check: Suppression list enforced across all senders
description: Hard bounces, complaints, and unsubscribes live in one suppression store consulted by every send path (app, marketing tool, one-off scripts).
why_it_matters: Re-mailing a complainer from a different tool is how reputations and legal compliance die

description: Subscribers with no opens/clicks in 6-12 months get a re-engagement sequence then removal; engagement-based sunset policy is documented.
why_it_matters: Mailing dead addresses tanks engagement metrics that inbox providers use to rank you

description: Email-sending signup/subscription endpoints have rate limiting and CAPTCHA-on-abuse so attackers can't weaponize your forms to bomb victims' inboxes.
why_it_matters: List-bombing victims mark you as spam in bulk and your forms become an abuse vector

description: Templates avoid URL shorteners, mismatched link text vs href, image-only bodies, and ALL-CAPS/spam-trigger subjects; total size stays under Gmail's ~102KB clipping limit.
why_it_matters: Content signals still feed spam filters; clipped mail also hides the unsubscribe link (an EM014 violation)

description: Provider enforces STARTTLS/TLS for outbound delivery; MTA-STS and TLS-RPT records published for the receiving side of your domain.
why_it_matters: Unencrypted email transit leaks content and increasingly costs deliverability trust

check: Sending secrets follow the env-secrets discipline
description: Provider API keys live in the secrets manager, scoped per environment, rotated on offboarding — never in code or templates (same rule as env-secrets-management.md).
why_it_matters: A leaked email API key lets an attacker send as your domain — reputational and phishing damage

check: Authentication verified with real tools not assumptions
description: After DNS setup and on a quarterly schedule: verify with a round-trip test (mail-tester.com or provider's checker) that SPF, DKIM, DMARC all pass from every stream.
why_it_matters: DNS drifts, providers change infrastructure, and silent auth failures look exactly like normal sending

check: Sender identity and address requirements met
description: Marketing mail includes the legally required sender identification (physical address where applicable) and honors regional rules (KVKK/CAN-SPAM/ePrivacy) for the audiences served.
why_it_matters: Missing sender-identity requirements is a direct legal exposure independent of deliverability
